title: Auth Middleware + Lazy User Provisioning
purpose: API middleware verifies JWT via IdentityProvider, lazily provisions identity.users on first authenticated request, and attaches auth context to the request. This is the PRIMARY user provisioning path; webhooks are reconciliation only.
architectural_constraints: First authenticated request provisions identity.users atomically
Webhook is reconciliation mechanism, not primary provisioning
JWT verification via JWKS, not Supabase SDK directly
Auth context attached to AsyncLocalStorage or request object
Correlation ID propagated from JWT or generated

deliverables.description: Verify JWT, lazy provision user, attach auth context
Atomic user creation on first auth request
Unit tests with mocked IdentityProvider and JWT

acceptance_criteria: Valid JWT → identity.users row exists (created if missing)
Invalid JWT → 401 with clear error code
Missing identity.users row → auto-created with auth_provider='supabase'
Auth context contains userId, email, authProvider
Correlation ID propagated from request header or generated
No Supabase SDK import outside infrastructure/auth/

failure_modes.scenario: JWT valid but identity.users creation fails
failure_modes.mitigation: Return 500, log error, do not cache failed state

failure_modes.scenario: Concurrent first requests race to create user
failure_modes.mitigation: UNIQUE constraint on id + idempotent INSERT ... ON CONFLICT
